mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of future earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a persistent, always-active drift effect independent of regime triggers.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-rate-of-change periods show no return advantage versus flat/rising periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month trailing low, this crosses a labor-market deterioration threshold historically associated with recession onset (Sahm-rule-like dynamic), triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the 0.3pp threshold is crossed, forward 60d SPX returns are non-negative, or hit rate falls below 55% in walk-forward backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth combined with a rising UNRATE indicates a genuine turn in labor demand rather than noise in either series alone, jointly signaling weakening consumer income growth and forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone reliably predicts the turn; the interaction of slowing payrolls and rising unemployment is required.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE up >0.1pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce positive median 20d forward returns, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either series alone is not statistically significant (p>0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above 3.5%), declines in HOUST reflect amplified interest-rate sensitivity of housing demand, serving as an early indicator of broader consumer retrenchment and predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In low-rate regimes the same HOUST decline carries little forward-return signal because housing is not the marginal transmission channel.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 3.5%
failure_condition: HOUST declines under high-FEDFUNDS conditions fail to precede negative 60d SPX returns in a majority of historical episodes, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from HOUST declines occurring in low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate production activity, which is quickly priced into equities as a signal of near-term earnings strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week. This is a continuous, always-on trend-following relationship.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5d SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign-agreement rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending, which supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month. Because RSAFS is a coincident demand indicator, its trend directly informs near-term equity risk appetite.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following positive RSAFS growth months are not significantly higher than those following negative growth months (t-test p>0.05) across a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy begins to materially compress valuation multiples and increase recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter. Below this threshold the rate level is not restrictive enough to override growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently at 3.78 percent, below trigger
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% show non-negative median 60d forward SPX returns, or hit rate for negative returns falls below 55% in walk-forward backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated CPIAUCSL inflation readings and a high FEDFUNDS level captures real policy restrictiveness; when nominal rates fail to keep pace with cooling inflation (implying rising real rates), equity markets react negatively within the next week as real financing costs bite before earnings can adjust. Neither CPI nor FEDFUNDS alone captures this real-rate squeeze.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains flat or rises, implying real rate increase > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: disinflationary period with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: Rising real-rate episodes (via CPI deceleration + flat/rising FEDFUNDS) show non-negative median 5d forward SPX returns, or the interaction effect's predictive power is not distinguishable from the FEDFUNDS-only single-series signal (p>0.10).
